question_text: Two sounds have the same frequency. Sound A has twice the amplitude of Sound B. Which statement is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Sound A will travel twice as far as Sound B

options.label: B
options.text: Sound A has a higher pitch than Sound B

options.label: C
options.text: Sound A is louder than Sound B, but they have the same pitch

options.label: D
options.text: Sound A travels faster through air than Sound B

explanation: Amplitude determines loudness, so Sound A (larger amplitude) is louder. Frequency determines pitch, and since both sounds have the same frequency, they have the same pitch. Speed of sound in a medium doesn't depend on amplitude or frequency.
